hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Attack Line A: 250 ft of 1¾-inch hose from Engine 181 pump panel discharge through the front entrance to a third-floor interior attack position
Attack Line B: 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose from Engine 181 rear discharge to a ground-level exterior attack position
nozzles: Attack Line A: ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle, 185 GPM @ 50 PSI
Attack Line B: ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle, 185 GPM @ 50 PSI
sceneElements.elevation: Attack Line A operates on the 3rd floor. Attack Line B operates from ground level.

problems.title: Interior Attack Elevation
problems.question: Attack Line A is operating on the 3rd floor. Use the elevation formula: height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft, and elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. What elevation pressure must be added for Line A?
formulaBreakdown: Elevation formula: height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft; elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft.
problems.instructorExplanation: The interior attack line is operating on the 3rd floor. Using the elevation formula, height = (3 - 1) × 10 ft = 20 ft, and elevation pressure = 20 × 0.5 = 10 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not treat the interior line as grade level. The hose enters the building and the attack is operating above ground level.

problems.title: Attack Line A Friction Loss
problems.question: Attack Line A is 250 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM. Using C = 15.5, what is the friction loss in Attack Line A?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Line A uses 1¾-inch hose, so C = 15.5.
Line A hose length = 250 ft = 2.5 hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Attack Line A is calculated from the engine discharge through the front entrance to the interior attack position. The friction loss rounds to 135 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add elevation or nozzle pressure when the question asks only for friction loss.

problems.title: Attack Line B Friction Loss
problems.question: Attack Line B is 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM. Using C = 15.5, what is the friction loss in Attack Line B?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Line B uses 1¾-inch hose, so C = 15.5.
Line B hose length = 200 ft = 2 hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Attack Line B is a ground-level exterior line. At 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM, the friction loss rounds to 105 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the Line A length for Line B. Each attack line must be calculated separately.

problems.title: Governing Attack Line
problems.question: Line A is 250 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI to the 3rd floor. Line B is 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI at ground level. Use the elevation formula for Line A: height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft, and elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. Which attack line governs the pump discharge pressure?
problems.inputLabel: Enter 1 for Line A or 2 for Line B
formulaBreakdown: Line A PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
problems.instructorExplanation: Line A governs because it requires the highest pump discharge pressure. It has both greater friction loss and elevation pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not choose the exterior line just because it is visibly flowing upward. The governing line is the line with the highest calculated pump discharge pressure.

problems.title: Required Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying Line A and Line B. Line A is 250 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI to the 3rd floor. Line B is 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI at ground level. Use FL = C × (GPM/100)² × (length/100), C = 15.5, and calculate the elevation for Line A using height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft and elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. What pump discharge pressure should Engine 181 set for the governing line?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 must be set for the highest-pressure flowing line. Line A requires 195 PSI because it includes friction loss, nozzle pressure, and elevation to the third-floor interior attack position.
problems.explainMistake: Do not average the two line pressures. Multiple lines supplied from the same pump are set to the highest required discharge pressure.
